methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $70M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Dust is a Series B-stage AI & Software company founded by Gabriel Hubert and Stanislas Polu that has raised $70M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 comparables (1 public, 8 private), including same-stage AI companies such as Safe Superintelligence, Physical Intelligence, World Labs, Nominal, and Photoroom.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression, using total raised as a proxy base value of $70M and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yields $133M. Peer-median triangulation across five same-sector, same-stage comparables produces $5.0B, with the peer set ranging from $500M (Photoroom) to $32.0B (Safe Superintelligence). The wide range reflects heterogeneity in AI frontier-tech positioning and investor appetite within the cohort.

Triangulating these two methods yields a low of $113M, median of $2.6B, and high of $5.8B. The substantial spread between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects structural uncertainty in how growth-stage AI companies are being valued relative to historical funding progression patterns. The peer-median estimate carries material weight given the relevance of the comparable set, though the extreme upper outlier (Safe Superintelligence at $32B) and lower outlier (Photoroom at $500M) suggest caution in point-estimate reliance. Key uncertainties include product traction metrics, runway, and whether Dust's positioning commands premium or discount relative to the observed peer valuations.
